acceptance_criteria: The release artifact verifier rejects missing, unexpected, unsafe, symlinked or policy-divergent package entries and validates the real npm tarball.
Installer V2 supports check, plan, apply, validate, confirm and restore with a durable transaction receipt, zero writes during planning and byte-preserved rollback.
Installer V2 stages the personal marketplace and plugin without modifying global Codex configuration, AGENTS.md or RTK.md.
The GitHub release workflow publishes only after the exact tarball and Installer V2 plan are verified, derives prerelease status from strict SemVer and remains retry-safe.
Package metadata and user-facing documentation consistently identify v0.12.0 and make Installer V2 the canonical self-service path.
Node.js 18, 20 and 24 release tests, the complete Node.js 24 suite, Python installer tests, static checks, dry-run packaging and real-package smoke validation pass before merge.
integrations: npm pack and npm CLI package verification
GitHub Actions release workflow and GitHub CLI
Codex personal marketplace and plugin installer
nfrs: Release verification must work on Node.js 18, 20 and 24 and installer tests must use the supported local Python runtime.
No shell evaluation, credential material or host-specific absolute path may enter the packaged artifact.
non_goals: Do not publish to npm, create a GitHub release or push a tag in this pull request.
Do not perform the local installation in this pull request; that requires a separate local-release choice.
Do not access production or secrets and do not modify global Codex configuration, AGENTS.md or RTK.md.
material_scope.objective: Ship v0.12.0 with policy-driven package verification, a reversible Installer V2, and a verified retry-safe GitHub release workflow, while keeping publication and local installation separate.
requirement_constraints: Package verification and workflow guards must be deterministic, policy-driven and fail closed.
Installer planning must be zero-write and restore must recover the exact pre-transaction state.
Preserve the portfolio provider-subject compatibility check while extending package.json release checks.
material_scope.scope: Ship v0.12.0 with policy-driven package verification, a reversible Installer V2, and a verified retry-safe GitHub release workflow, while keeping publication and local installation separate.

approval.summary: Antonio ha approvato il requisito release 0.12.0, i controlli, le esclusioni e il tetto massimo di autonomia; ogni PR e installazione locale conserva comunque una scelta separata.

scope.principle: A human approval applies only to the specific artifact or decision shown to the user before the approval.
